client.name: Orschell Excavating
client.context: Likely sells equipment parts, attachments, aggregate/materials, or branded gear for construction/contracting industry

posting.summary: Implement backend features for existing e-commerce website: auth, product catalog, inventory, order processing, payment gateway, REST API, optimization, database improvements

explicit.demoRealization: Real signup/login with JWT; account page with profile + order history; seeded demo admin and customer accounts with credentials documented
Admin CMS: create/edit/delete products, categories, images, pricing, detailed specs; full CRUD interface
Stock counts decremented on order; low-stock flags in admin; admin stock level edits; real persistence
Full flow: cart add/update/remove, checkout, order creation; admin order status pipeline (pending→processing→shipped→delivered)
Mock checkout form (clearly labeled 'Demo / Mock Payment' in UI); simulated success/decline; no real charge processing
Documented REST endpoints: products, cart, orders, auth, admin operations; real backend service
Pagination on product lists, indexed database queries, client-side caching where beneficial; noted in deck
Proper PostgreSQL schema with foreign keys, indexes, constraints; migrations + seed data; production-ready structure

implicit: Posting asks for 'examples of similar projects, estimated timeline, and approach' — must be included in cover letter
Frontend must accompany backend (full e-commerce UX, not just API)

recommended: Express or similar Node web framework
Database migrations tooling (e.g., Knex, TypeORM, Prisma)
React or similar for frontend UI

designDirection.doNotCopy: michaelwegter.com portfolio aesthetic (dark gallery-wall, mustard/cyan, Space Grotesk)

industrialStorefrontGuidance.palette: Steel grey, safety-amber/orange, asphalt black, hi-vis accents
industrialStorefrontGuidance.imagery: Heavy-equipment, utilitarian dense product grid, contractor-supply aesthetic
industrialStorefrontGuidance.tone: Rugged, professional, utilitarian; built for busy contractors

scope.featureRichnessInterpretation: Production-quality, several genuinely working features, real persistence, polish. Not a prototype.

architecture.backend: Node.js + TypeScript + PostgreSQL service on Surface (matches client's exact stack); exposed via Flask bridge blueprint at /demos/orschell-excavating-e-commerce-full/api/
architecture.frontend: Self-contained React app (Vite or CDN) calling REST API; embedded in michaelwegter.com/public/demos/<slug>/
architecture.fallback: If Surface Postgres infeasible: Express+TS + node:sqlite with Postgres-shaped schema; call out in deck that production target is Postgres

heroFeatures: Customer storefront: product browsing + cart + checkout (mocked payment)

hardRequirements.demoAccounts: Seed admin account (for CMS demo) + demo customer account (for checkout flow); credentials recorded in build report for deploy-test and live use
hardRequirements.mockPayment: Explicitly labeled 'Demo / Mock Payment' or 'Sandbox' in UI; no real charges
hardRequirements.adminCMS: Fully functional admin interface for product, inventory, and order management — not a wireframe
hardRequirements.persistence: Real PostgreSQL database with proper schema, FKs, indexes; cart/orders survive page refresh
hardRequirements.noConsoleErrors: Demo loads cleanly on first paint; passes Lighthouse accessibility baseline

qualityGates: All 8 posting requirements addressed in cover letter AND present as working features in demo or documented in deck
Demo builds, no console errors on first paint; /demos/<slug>/ resolves locally; live /work-samples/<slug> returns 200
Demo link appears in BOTH cover letter and deck
Cohesive bespoke industrial design (not portfolio aesthetic); zero em-dashes or en-dashes in all prose
Cover letter includes: timeline estimate, technical approach/architecture, 2-3 similar e-commerce or full-stack project examples (posting explicitly requests)
REST API endpoints documented (in demo or attached README)
postingAsks: Examples of similar e-commerce or web application projects

constraints.filesTouch: ~12 files max (backend service + frontend app + bridge blueprint)
constraints.postingScope: Existing e-commerce site — but Michael's directive is to build both frontend and backend from scratch, production-ready, showcasing all features
constraints.paymentGateway: Must be mocked; no real Stripe/PayPal integration (too much scope), but UI must simulate a realistic flow

riskFlags: Surface Postgres setup may be time-expensive; have node:sqlite fallback ready (schema is portable to Postgres)
Full storefront + admin + inventory + order mgmt = broad scope at feature-richness 10; planner must fence hero (storefront checkout) vs supporting (admin CMS, inventory edge cases) strictly
Demo accounts must be seeded and credentials stable for deploy-test and live demo

handoff.produced: brief.json — structured parsing of 8 explicit requirements, technical stack (Node+TS+Postgres), industrial design direction, hard requirements (admin CMS, mocked payment, real DB), quality gates, and risk flags
handoff.decisions: Feature richness 10; real Node+TS+Postgres backend on Surface via Flask bridge; full admin CMS required; payment mocked and labeled; industrial contractor-supply aesthetic (not portfolio look)
handoff.nextNeeds: Planner: refine design direction per research, fence hero vs supporting, estimate timeline for proposal cover letter, decide Postgres vs sqlite fallback architecture
handoff.risks: Scope is large (full storefront + admin + inventory + orders); planner must tightly scope hero features to fit 3-min build time and ~12-file limit. Surface Postgres availability (fallback: sqlite with note in deck).
